leg: A — shading + borders fidelity (spec §8.3, slice 2; roundtrip variant)
plannedMethod: Word re-save (oracle roundtrip verb) of the Task-12 fixture /tmp/wc-s2-roundtrip.docx (copied to tests/fixtures/.oracle-rt-in.docx because roundtrip SAVES and /tmp is sandbox-blocked for save-as), then clone reimport of WORD's output asserting shading fill FFFF00 + borders.bottom survive.
whatActuallyHappened: The roundtrip could not run this session: Word's document-open machinery degraded to fully non-functional partway through the run (the full Task-12 fixture, a fresh-named copy, AND a list-free derivative all timed out at 540 s opens; diagnosis in 2026-06-06-slice2-verdicts.json -> sessionFindings). Word's authoring + save-as path kept working, so the validation was inverted: shading + bottom border were authored in REAL Word via the object model and the clone imported WORD's native output.

wordAuthoredLeg.method: make new document; set background pattern color index of (shading of text object of paragraph 1) to yellow; set line style of (get border (text object of paragraph 2) which border border bottom) to line style single; save as ... tests/fixtures/oracle-word-shading.docx; close by name-verified ordinal; count 0 before/after.
wordAuthoredLeg.xmlEquivalence: Attribute-for-attribute IDENTICAL forms in both directions (only serialization order differs). Word's WdColorIndex 'yellow' is FFFF00 — the same hex the clone's shading palette pick stores.

expected.check: shading fill FFFF00 survives a Word-native cycle
expected.expected: clone model shading.fill 'FFFF00' + painted background after importing WORD's file
expected.actual: shading {val clear, color auto, fill FFFF00}; painted rgb(255,255,0)
expected.verdict: PASS (Word-authored -> clone import; the clone's own export uses the identical w:shd form Word writes)

expected.expected: clone model borders.bottom {val single} + painted border-bottom after importing WORD's file
expected.actual: borders.bottom {val single, size 4, space 1, color auto}; painted border-bottom
expected.verdict: PASS (Word-authored -> clone import; identical w:pBdr form both directions; size 4 eighth-points and space 1 match the clone's Word-default border constants in commands.js applyBorder)

expected.check: Word RE-SAVE of the clone's file (the literal roundtrip verb)
expected.actual: UNVERIFIED-THIS-SESSION — Word could not open any new file this session (environment; not content-correlated). Re-run after Word relaunch.

verdict: PASS on the fidelity question (identical OOXML forms both directions + clone correctly imports Word-native shading/borders), with the literal Word-re-save cycle deferred to a healthy Word session.
followUp: After Word relaunch: node scripts/oracle/word-oracle.js roundtrip $(pwd)/tests/fixtures/oracle-rt-in.docx $(pwd)/tests/fixtures/oracle-rt-out.docx (non-dot copies already staged, untracked), then reimport oracle-rt-out.docx in the clone and assert shading/borders.
pidSafety: count 0 before/after; only our authored doc was saved/closed (name-verified); Word never quit.
